title: veronica longifolia subsp. pseudolongifolia
intro: Veronica longifolia subsp. pseudolongifolia is a lesser-known subspecies of the Veronica longifolia plant, commonly referred to as the long-leaved speedwell. It is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to provide relief from various health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions, and may also help to reduce the risk of infections and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Veronica longifolia subsp. pseudolongifolia is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits.
